questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the Indian National Movement, the 'Ring Theatre' in Lucknow is historically significant for which of the following reasons?

options.text: It was the venue where the Lucknow Pact was signed between the Congress and the Muslim League.
It served as the primary location for the trial proceedings of the Kakori Conspiracy Case.
It was the site where the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA) was formally founded.
It was the headquarters of the United Provinces provincial government during the 1920 capital shift.

questions.explanation: The Ring Theatre in Lucknow was the specific location where the Kakori Trial Case was held. This trial resulted in the death sentences of key revolutionaries like Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaq Ullah Khan.
questions.takeaway: Specific historical venues like the Ring Theatre are often associated with major political trials in UPSC questions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: KAKORI TRIAL CASE - RING THEATRE in Lucknow. Death sentence - Ram Prasad Bismil, Ashfaq Ullah Khan, Rajendra Nath Lahiri, Roshan Singh
